data.title: Union Pacific works the White House as it looks for regulatory clearance on the Norfolk Southern deal
data.description:
Union Pacific Corp. (NYSE:UNP) CEO Jim Vena discussed the impact of the company's proposed $72 billion acquisition of Norfolk Southern Corp. (NYSE:NSC) with President Donald Trump in the White House on Friday. Reports indicate the meeting went well for Union Pacific (NYSE:UNP).

Earlier in the week, Vena indicated that senior administration officials understand the value of what the company is proposing, and they think it's an absolute win for the U.S. The deal is opposed by some transportation industry watchers due to the impact on competition and freight costs.

Union Pacific (UNP) is looking at acquiring Norfolk Southern in an $85 billion stock-and-cash transaction that would create the first coast-to-coast U.S. railroad by connecting over 50,000 miles of track across 43 states. The deal values Norfolk Southern (NYSE:NSC) at $320 per share and gives its shareholders cash plus 27% of the combined entity. The deal is seen as boosting supply chain efficiencies and regaining market share from trucking companies. The agreement received unanimous board approval but still requires Surface Transportation Board review. If approved, the deal is not expected to close until the early part of 2027.

Norfolk Southern (NSC) edged 0.6% higher in afternoon trading, while Union Pacific (UNP) was down 1.1%.

Market caps in the rails industry: Union Pacific (UNP) $127.2 billion, Canadian Pacific Kansas City Limited (CP) $69.2 billion, Norfolk Southern (NSC) $61.9 billion, CSX Corp (CSX) $60.8 billion, and Canadian National Railway (CNI) $58.5 billion. Berkshire Hathaway's (BRK.A) (BRK.B) BNSF Railway is estimated to have a value of around $80 billion.

data.title: Jamf seesaws on report it's exploring a sale
data.description:
Device management software firm Jamf (NASDAQ:JAMF) quickly rose and is now down 2% after a report that it's exploring a sale. 

Jamf, which is backed by private equity firm Vista Equity with a 34% stake, is working with bankers at Citi on the sale process, according to a  Reuters report on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter. The company has been taking offers from potential buyers in recent months. Vista would exit its position as part of the sale. 

Jamf (NASDAQ:JAMF) has a market value of ~$1.4 billion. Its shares have dropped 23% this year.

Jamf didn't respond to Reuters request for comment.

data.title: U.S. stocks mixed as investors look for direction with Fed decision looming
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were mixed on Friday, after the S&P 500 (SP500), the Nasdaq (COMP:IND), and the Dow (DJI) achieved new record closes a day before.

Now investors await the Federal Reserve's decision on interest rate cuts, with the markets pricing odds of a 25-basis-point rate cut next week at 92.5%, according to the CME FedWatch Tool. 

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.1% in afternoon trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.5% and hitting intraday highs, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.4%

“If the Fed doesn't cut rates multiple times, the economy, especially the job market, will get into even more trouble. If the Fed cuts rates multiple times, the risk of inflation rising again painfully is imminent. In short: the Fed is trapped,” said Marc-André Fongern, former Deutsche Bank and Goldman Sachs investment banker.

In addition, the U.S. Consumer Sentiment Index fell to 55.4 in September from 58.2 in August, missing the 54.9 consensus.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 4 basis points to 4.07%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 3.57%.

Among the top gainers on Friday, Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) and Paramount Skydance (PSKY) were +19.1% and +5.9% respectively on the announcement that the latter is preparing a majority cash bid for the entertainment giant.

U.S. stocks set another record closing on Thursday, with the Dow Jones (DJI) reaching the 46,000 level for the first time, after the consumer inflation report came higher on a monthly basis in August, while core CPI was steady.

More on markets:

data.title: Sunstone Hotel parts worth more than whole - analyst
data.description:
Sunstone Hotel (NYSE:SHO) should consider a sale as the sum of its parts is worth more than its whole, according to a Truist analyst, after an investor called on the company to consider selling itself. 

Earlier on Friday, Tarsadia, which owns a 3.4% stake in Sunstone Hotel (NYSE:SHO), called on the hotel REIT to to sell itself or liquidate its assets. Tarsadia also wants the real estate investment trust to appoint new directors to its 8-member board.

"As of yesterday's close, SHO shares were trading at 12.7x 2025 EV/EBITDA and 12.3x 2026E," Truist analyst Charles Scholes, who has a hold rating and $10 price target on Sunstone, wrote in a note on Friday. "However, we believe the underlying assets are worth at minimum 14x which would be worth ~$12/share; each 100 bps."

Likely buyers of the individual assets include private equity and/or high net worth individuals/families/sovereign wealth looking for trophy assets, according to Scholes. He  sees a smaller pool of company buyers as high interest rates create higher thresholds for go-private transactions. 

"This activism is not 100% a surprise given share performance for SHO and hotel REITS overall and a related disconnect between underlying asset valuation and equity valuation," Scholes added.

data.title: Amazon eyes EV options besides Rivian with GM's BrightDrop in the running
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) has begun testing Chevrolet (NYSE:GM) BrightDrop electric vans for its delivery fleet, putting pressure on shares of Rivian Automotive (RIVN).

The e-commerce giant confirmed to Bloomberg that, as part of its goal to have 100,000 EVs on the road by 2030, it is evaluating options beyond its existing fleet of Rivian (RIVN) delivery vans. That target suggests Amazon (AMZN) may look to diversify suppliers rather than rely solely on Rivian (RIVN).

Amazon (AMZN) originally struck a deal with Rivian (RIVN) in September 2019 to purchase 100,000 electric vans, a commitment paired with a “significant” investment in the EV maker. But so far, Rivian (RIVN) has delivered just 30,000 vehicles, with its CEO projecting a meaningful production ramp only by 2026–2027.

To bridge the gap, Amazon (AMZN) is testing vehicles from other automakers, including GM (NYSE:GM), Ford (F), Stellantis (STLA), and Mercedes-Benz (MBGAF, MBGYY).

For GM (NYSE:GM), winning an Amazon (AMZN) contract would mark a major boost for its young commercial EV business. The company folded its BrightDrop division into the GM Evolve unit last year, which supplies fleet vehicles to businesses and government agencies. BrightDrop vans, with a range of up to 272 miles, are already in use by FedEx (FDX) and several public-sector fleets.

data.title: Via Transportation trades slightly below its pricing level in IPO opening
data.description:
Via Transportation (NYSE:VIA) opened trading at $44.00 after pricing its IPO at $46 per share. The offering includes 10.7 million Class A shares, consisting of 7.1 million from the company and 3.6 million from selling stockholders

The company, which was founded in New York City in 2012 by Daniel Ramot and Oren Shoval, started as a ridesharing service on Manhattan's Upper East Side, letting commuters book shared rides for a fixed fee. The company quickly expanded citywide and then internationally. Since then, Via (NYSE:VIA) shifted focus from operating its own ride-sharing platform to providing its advanced technology to cities, transit agencies, and private operators. Via Transportation (NYSE:VIA) serves 689 customers in over 30 countries in North America and Europe.

Notable investors in Via Transportation include Shell (SHEL), BlackRock (BLK), and Janus Henderson Investors.

In an SEC filing, Via Transportation (VIA) said it is addressing a striking gap in the $545 billion global public transportation market. 

"While billions of people across the globe rely on public transportation, this critical form of mobility has yet to meaningfully benefit from recent advances in technology. Buses still follow fixed routes and schedules planned years, if not decades ago, regardless of actual demand for their service. We can track our pizza from the moment it leaves the oven, but parents of more than 25 million children in the United States have no way of knowing when their child’s school bus will arrive. Some of our most vulnerable citizens, who depend on paratransit to access critical medical care, have no alternative to cumbersome phone reservations that must be made a day or more in advance."

The company says it can play a key role in fixing the transportation issues.

data.title: AstraZeneca follows Merck’s pullback in Britain: report
data.description:
AstraZeneca (NASDAQ:AZN) has become the latest leading drugmaker to reconsider its investments in Britain, as the Anglo-Swedish pharma giant has paused plans for a £200M (~$271M) investment in its Cambridge research site, following a similar move by Merck (NYSE:MRK), Reuters reported.

The investment, which was set to create about 1,000 jobs in the U.K., is the latest to face discontinuation after the FTSE-100 company decided to scrap a £450M expansion at a vaccine production plant in Northern England in January.

The decision will mean that none of its planned investments originally announced in March 2024 are currently advancing in Britain.

"We constantly reassess the investment needs of our company and can confirm our expansion in Cambridge is paused. We have no further comment to make," an AstraZeneca (NASDAQ:AZN) spokesperson noted.

The spokesperson was responding to a question about speculation regarding its investments after Merck (NYSE:MRK) announced on Wednesday that it has canceled plans for a £1B research center in London, citing a challenging business environment in the country.

Both announcements precede U.S. President Donald Trump’s second state visit to the U.K. next week. Trump has often threatened to impose tariffs on pharmaceutical products in a bid to encourage leading drugmakers to invest in the U.S. In July, AstraZeneca (NASDAQ:AZN) pledged to make investments worth $50B in the U.S. by 2030.
